assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 42-63 contain '## Security Configuration' with subsections '### Product Lifecycle' (Product pages URL: https://access.example.com/product-lifecycle, Jira version prefix: MYPRODUCT, Vulnerability issue type ID: 10200, Component label pattern: pscomponent:, VEX Justification custom field: customfield_12345), '### Version Streams' (2.1.x stream with all columns), and '### Source Repositories' (backend and frontend-ui). All match the reference file exactly.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 97-115) checks Security Configuration for completeness and concludes with '**Result: Security Configuration is up to date.**' at line 115. It also verifies all sub-sections: Product Lifecycle (all 5 fields checked), Version Streams (1 stream configured), and Source Repositories (2 repositories configured).

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md line 19 shows: '| Security Configuration | No change | Fully populated — Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories all complete |'. The summary on line 6 states: '**Project Configuration is up to date — no changes needed.**' and line 23 shows 'Files Modified' as 'None.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 97-115) detects Security Configuration is already present and fully populated, marking all fields with checkmarks and concluding 'Security Configuration is up to date.' The changes-log.md confirms 'No change' for Security Configuration. There is no evidence of an opt-in prompt being offered in any output file. The discovery log shows detection of the existing section, not a prompt to add it.

assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 4-8 show the Repository Registry table with two entries: '| backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/backend |' and '| frontend-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/frontend-ui |'. These match the reference file exactly.
